algorithms.title: Behavioral Emergency Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for safely assessing and de-escalating a patient in behavioral crisis.
steps: Assess scene safety from a distance before approaching: weapons, exits, patient affect.
If a credible safety concern exists, involve law enforcement per local protocol before approaching.
Once the scene is safe, approach calmly and apply the SEA-3 framework: Safety, Emotional state, Appearance, Activity, Speech.
Apply de-escalation technique: open-ended questions, non-threatening posture and distance, calm even tone, avoid arguing with delusional content.
If de-escalation succeeds: complete assessment and transport with a continued calm approach.
If de-escalation fails and the patient poses immediate danger to self or others: apply restraint per local protocol as a last resort, with appropriate monitoring and documentation.
If the patient refuses care: assess capacity, follow informed-refusal protocol, and ensure continuity of care to avoid abandonment.
